title: chrysothamnus parryi subsp. vulcanicus
intro: Chrysothamnus parryi subsp. vulcanicus, also known as the Santa Cruz Island chamise, is a subspecies of the Chrysothamnus parryi plant, which is a shrub native to the Channel Islands of California. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Chrysothamnus parryi subsp. vulcanicus include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tea or salve, have been used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
